This belongs to me.

hint: Say your phone has no battery

The road is blocked.

Turn left at the junction.

Let us pray together.

Come back very soon.

Be strong and brave.
Trust in God always.

Watch out, danger ahead.

Which way should I go?
Follow me this way.
Pray for our journey.
The market is open today.

God bless you and your family.
Travel safely my friend.
I will remember you always.

Listen to me carefully.

The rain will come soon.
My cow needs water now.
He is a good man.
We arrived safely today.
I was born in Freetown.
She lives near the mosque.
The children are playing outside in the yard.
I was born in Freetown and grew up by the coast.
We traveled from Conakry to Labé through the hills.
The market in Bo town is very busy on Saturdays.
hint: Talk about a busy market in Bo

english_text: My grandmother lives in Kenema near the forest.
The ferry from Banjul goes across the river every morning.
hint: Talk about a ferry in Banjul

english_text: I studied at university in Freetown for four years.
The road from Makeni to Kabala passes through the mountains.
hint: Talk about a road in Sierra Leone

english_text: My uncle moved to Dakar to find work last year.
hint: Talk about going to Dakar for work

english_text: We crossed the border from Guinea into Sierra Leone at Pamelap.
The port of Freetown handles ships from many countries.
Kankan is a big city in eastern Guinea with many traders.
My father grew up in Tambacounda before moving to the city.
The cattle market in Farafenni draws people from all over Gambia.
hint: Talk about a cattle market in Gambia

english_text: She took the bus from Serrekunda to Brikama early in the morning.
hint: Talk about bus travel in Gambia

english_text: The hospital in Makeni serves people from many surrounding villages.
hint: Talk about a hospital in Makeni

english_text: We fished at the lagoon near Bonthe Island last weekend.
The gold mines near Koidu bring many workers from different places.
Port Loko is about an hour's drive from Freetown by road.
hint: Talk about distance to Port Loko

english_text: My sister got married in Kindia and now lives there with her husband.
hint: Talk about someone married in Kindia

english_text: The border town of Rosso connects Senegal and Mauritania by ferry.
Kolda is a major town in southern Senegal close to the Gambia border.
The rice fields near Ziguinchor are flooded during the rainy season.
hint: Talk about rice farming near Ziguinchor

english_text: We drove through Labé on our way to Mamou during the festival.
The weekly market in Basse draws traders from Mali and Senegal.
hint: Talk about Basse market in Gambia

english_text: Nzérékoré is the largest city in the forest region of Guinea.
My brother works on the docks in Conakry near the presidential palace.
We prayed at the old mosque in the center of Kaolack.
The school in Moyamba district was built by the community together.
Siguiri is known for gold mining along the river in Guinea.
hint: Talk about gold mining in Siguiri

english_text: The bridge over the Gambia River connects the north and south banks.
hint: Talk about a river bridge in Gambia

english_text: We spent three days in Mopti visiting the old mud mosques.
hint: Talk about visiting Mopti in Mali

english_text: The road to Kailahun used to take a whole day to travel.
hint: Talk about a long road journey

english_text: Faranah is where the Niger River begins its long journey to the sea.
hint: Talk about the Niger River source

english_text: My cousin runs a small shop in Thiès near the train station.
hint: Talk about a shop in Thiès

english_text: The children from Pujehun district walk far to reach their school.
hint: Talk about children walking to school

english_text: Kanifing is now a very crowded suburb just outside the capital Banjul.
We passed through Boké on our way to the bauxite mining area.
The women of Vélingara are famous for their beautiful woven cloth.
hint: Talk about women weavers in Vélingara

english_text: Koinadugu district is known for its cool highland climate in Sierra Leone.
hint: Talk about cool highlands in Sierra Leone

english_text: Saint-Louis was once the capital of French West Africa long ago.
The fishing boats leave from Yawri Bay before the sunrise every morning.
Georgetown on the Gambia River is a quiet but beautiful old town.
Bambali village in Guinea is surrounded by mountains and green valleys.
hint: Talk about a village in the mountains

english_text: My parents traveled from Kayes to Bamako looking for better opportunities.
The ferry to Lungi airport crosses the estuary from central Freetown.
hint: Talk about crossing to the airport

english_text: Ségou is an important city on the Niger River known for its pottery.
The children swam in the clear water of the Rokel River near Makali.
hint: Talk about children swimming in a river

english_text: There are beautiful beaches along the coast between Freetown and Tokeh.
The border between Sierra Leone and Liberia passes through thick forest.
Mamou is an important crossroads town in the middle of Guinea.
hint: Talk about Mamou as a crossroads

english_text: The university hospital in Conakry is the biggest in the whole country.
Sikasso in southern Mali is known for its fruit and rich farmland.
We arrived in Banjul after a long journey by bus from Tambacounda.
hint: Talk about a long bus journey

english_text: The iron ore from Tonkolili is transported by train down to the coast.
The old market in Freetown called Big Market has been there for centuries.
Gao in northern Mali sits on the edge of the Sahara Desert.
hint: Talk about Gao near the desert

english_text: If you run away from a problem, it will follow you home and knock on your door.
hint: Problems you avoid will come back to you

english_text: The hand that gives is always higher than the hand that receives.
A tree that bends in the wind does not break like one that stands stiff.
When the lion roars the loudest, it is often the most hungry and desperate.
hint: Loud anger often comes from weakness

english_text: The child who is always sheltered from rain never learns to value dry days.
hint: Hardship teaches you to appreciate good things

english_text: A wise man listens more than he speaks, for words are the seeds of action.
The goat that has no friend must graze at the edge of the herd alone.
hint: Having no friends leaves you isolated

english_text: Rain does not ask permission before it falls on the farmer's fields.
hint: Some things happen whether you're ready or not

english_text: The path made by the first traveler is easy for all who come behind him.
hint: Those who go first make it easier for others

english_text: When two elephants fight, it is the grass beneath them that gets hurt.
A person who has no village has no name and no one to call family.
You cannot tell a hungry man to be patient until tomorrow for his food.
hint: Do not delay urgent needs of others

english_text: The ear that does not listen to advice will hear the sound of regret.
hint: Not taking advice leads to regret

english_text: A sharp knife that cuts only its owner is more dangerous than useful.
No matter how long the night lasts, the morning always comes after.
The farmer who plants in the dry season prays for the rain to come.
hint: Hard work paired with hope and prayer

english_text: A single hand cannot tie a bundle, but two hands make it easy work.
Speak the truth even if your voice shakes and your knees feel weak.
hint: Always speak truth even when afraid

english_text: A proud man and a foolish man often walk the same road to ruin.
hint: Pride and foolishness lead to the same end

english_text: The cow that stands alone far from the herd is the first one eaten.
hint: Safety comes from being with your group

english_text: A small fire can burn down the whole forest if no one tends to it.
hint: Small problems grow if you ignore them

english_text: Even the mightiest river started as a small trickle from a spring.
The monkey does not mock the fish for not being able to climb trees.
hint: Do not mock others for their different skills

english_text: A bird that flies too high and forgets the branch will fall to earth.
hint: Do not forget your roots when you succeed

english_text: The one who plants shade trees knows they will not sit under them alone.
An empty bag cannot stand upright on its own without support from inside.
hint: You need substance to stand strong

english_text: Words are like eggs, once they drop from your mouth they cannot go back.
Do not point at your parents' house with your left hand in anger.
hint: Always respect your parents and home

english_text: The night hides many things but the sun always reveals the truth.
A child who listens to their elders saves themselves years of hard lessons.
hint: Wisdom from elders saves you from mistakes

english_text: The hyena laughs loudest after the lion has done all the difficult work.
hint: Some people enjoy what others worked hard for

english_text: It takes a whole village to raise one good child in this world.
hint: Children need a whole community to grow well

english_text: A guest who stays too long in your house becomes a burden.
hint: Know when to leave; too long is too much

english_text: The traveler who asks questions does not get lost on the long road.
hint: Ask for help to find your way

english_text: When the morning dew disappears it does not mean the grass was not wet.
hint: Something can be true even when the evidence is gone

english_text: A person's character is more valuable than all the gold and silver combined.
hint: Good character is worth more than riches

english_text: The stone that rolls around does not grow moss and stays clean.
What the elder saw sitting down, the young cannot see standing up.
hint: Elders have wisdom the young haven't gained yet

english_text: The sweetest water often comes from the deepest and hardest wells.
hint: The best things require the most effort

english_text: Until the lion learns to write, the hunter's story will always be told.
hint: Those with power tell their side of history

english_text: A person without knowledge is like a tree without roots in the dry season.
hint: Knowledge gives you stability and strength

english_text: Do not look where you fell but look where you slipped on the way.
hint: Find the cause of problems not just the result

english_text: Rain falling at night is a blessing that needs no umbrella to receive.
hint: Hidden blessings come without you knowing

english_text: The dog that barks at the moon does not prevent the night from passing.
A child raised without discipline is like a garden without a fence around it.
hint: Children need boundaries to grow well

english_text: The bones of your grandparents lie in this earth, so respect this ground.
hint: Respect the land of your ancestors

english_text: You do not teach a grown elephant how to use its trunk to drink.
hint: Do not give unwanted advice to the experienced

english_text: Patience is the mother of all virtues and the father of all success.
hint: Being patient leads to everything good

english_text: A man who chases two rabbits at the same time catches neither one.
hint: Focus on one thing to succeed

english_text: The forest knows every secret but the trees never speak to strangers.
hint: Nature holds secrets that are not easily shared

english_text: When you wake in the morning, thank God before you speak to anyone else.
hint: Give thanks to God first thing in the morning

english_text: Bismillah, I begin this work in the name of the most merciful God.
hint: Start work by saying God's name

english_text: Alhamdulillah, God has given us another blessed day to live and breathe.
hint: Thank God for another day of life

english_text: Inshallah, we will all meet again at the next prayer time if God wills.
hint: Say you will meet again if God wills

english_text: The imam called us to the mosque for evening prayers as the sun set.
hint: Describe being called to evening prayer

english_text: We washed our hands and feet before entering the mosque for Friday prayer.
After the morning prayer, my father always reads quietly from the holy book.
hint: Describe reading holy book after prayer

english_text: The month of Ramadan is a time for fasting, prayer, and giving to others.
We broke our fast together as a family when the evening call to prayer came.
The holy book tells us to care for the poor, the orphan, and the traveler.
hint: Recite a religious teaching about helping others

english_text: Allahu Akbar, God is greater than anything in this world or the next.
We gave charity to the poor before leaving the house on the morning of Eid.
hint: Describe giving charity on Eid day

english_text: The night of power in Ramadan is better than a thousand months of worship.
hint: Describe the importance of Laylat al-Qadr

english_text: May God protect our family, our cattle, and our crops from all evil.
hint: Pray for protection of family and livelihood

english_text: We recited the opening chapter of the holy book before beginning the journey.
The children go to the Quranic school every morning before the regular school.
hint: Describe children going to Quranic school

english_text: May God reward you with good things for your kindness toward our family.
We slaughtered a ram and shared the meat with neighbors on the day of Eid.
The pilgrimage to Mecca is a dream that every good Muslim hopes to fulfill.
hint: Talk about the dream of Hajj

english_text: God does not burden any soul with more than it is able to carry.
Seek knowledge even if you have to travel as far as China to find it.
hint: Share a saying about seeking knowledge

english_text: Make your prayers, keep your fast, and give charity and God will bless you.
hint: Describe the pillars of good Muslim practice

english_text: We listened as the scholar explained the meaning of the verse after prayers.
hint: Describe learning from a religious scholar

english_text: Every morning before the sun rises, the muezzin calls us to the first prayer.
hint: Describe the early morning call to prayer

english_text: God gave us two ears and one mouth so we could listen twice as much.
hint: Share a wisdom saying about listening

english_text: I asked God for patience when my heart felt heavy and my burdens great.
The holy book is a guide for all people in all places and all times.
hint: Describe the holy book as a guide

english_text: Thank God for the rain that fell last night on our dry and thirsty fields.
hint: Thank God for rain on the crops

english_text: We pray five times every day because prayer keeps the heart at peace.
hint: Explain why Muslims pray five times daily

english_text: When you say Alhamdulillah for everything, God increases your blessings more.
hint: Explain the blessing of giving thanks

english_text: The old teacher said the greatest jihad is the struggle against your own desires.
hint: Share a teaching about inner struggle

english_text: God forgives those who truly repent and turn back to him in sincerity.
Recite the Fatiha for the soul of the one who has passed from this world.
We stayed up through the night praying during the last ten days of Ramadan.
A person who prays is never truly alone even in the deepest dark forest.
The prophets were sent to show us how to live a good and honest life.
hint: Talk about the purpose of prophets

english_text: Do good deeds quietly without seeking praise and God will reward you greatly.
hint: Share teaching about doing good secretly

english_text: A kind word is charity and a smile at your brother's face is also charity.
hint: Share a saying about simple acts of kindness

english_text: Do not waste the water you use for washing, it is a gift from God above.
Say Bismillah before you eat and Alhamdulillah when you have finished eating.
The young boy who memorized the whole Quran was celebrated by the whole village.
hint: Describe celebrating a child who memorized Quran

english_text: We gathered on the night of Eid to sing nasheeds and praise the prophet.
God sees every act of kindness even when no human eye is watching you.
hint: Remind that God sees all good deeds

english_text: My mother prays for us every morning before we leave the house for work.
The elders say that a house with prayer in it will never truly fall apart.
hint: Share elder wisdom about prayer protecting homes

english_text: We give thanks to God for the harvest and share food with all the village.
Before the herd drinks, the lead cow always tests the water at the edge carefully.
hint: Say the lead cow checks water before others drink

english_text: The rains came late this year and the grass did not grow tall enough for grazing.
hint: Say rain was late so grass is short

english_text: My grandfather moved his cattle from the lowlands to the hills when the dry season started.
hint: Say cattle moved to hills in dry season

english_text: The young bulls are separated from the herd until they are old enough to breed.
hint: Say young bulls are kept apart

english_text: We milk the cows twice a day, once in the morning and once in the evening.
A good herder knows each animal by its face, its voice, and its walk.
hint: Say a good herder knows each animal

english_text: The well dried up last month so we have to walk further to find water now.
hint: Say the well dried and you walk farther

english_text: During the harmattan, the cattle suffer from the dry wind and the dusty air.
hint: Describe cattle suffering in the dry harmattan season

english_text: We mark our cattle with a hot iron so we can identify them in the herd.
hint: Describe branding cattle to identify them

english_text: The shepherd boys sing to the goats to keep them calm during thunderstorms.
hint: Say boys sing to calm goats in storms

english_text: My father taught me to read the sky for signs of rain before moving the herd.
hint: Say you learn to predict rain from the sky

english_text: When the grass in the north runs out, we head south along the old migration path.
The fresh milk from the cow this morning will be made into butter and yogurt.
hint: Describe what to do with fresh morning milk

english_text: A cow that gives no milk is still useful for plowing and carrying heavy loads.
hint: Say even non-milking cows have uses

english_text: We traded two goats for a sack of millet and some salt at the market.
hint: Describe trading livestock at the market

english_text: The newborn calf stood on wobbly legs and called out to its mother nearby.
hint: Describe a newborn calf finding its mother

english_text: In our tradition, a man's wealth is counted by the number of cattle he owns.
hint: Explain cattle as wealth in tradition

english_text: The river is swollen now but by February it will be a thin dry stream.
hint: Describe how the river changes with seasons

english_text: We built a thorn fence around the cattle pen to keep the leopards out at night.
hint: Describe protecting cattle from predators at night

english_text: The elder said the best pasture in the whole region is three days walk to the east.
hint: Say an elder pointed out distant good pasture

english_text: When the hyena howled close to camp last night, the cattle became restless and noisy.
hint: Describe cattle reacting to a hyena at night

english_text: My mother churned the milk in the calabash for a long time before it turned to butter.
hint: Describe making butter from milk in a calabash

english_text: The old cow with the crooked horn always leads the others to water without fail.
hint: Describe an old lead cow guiding the herd

english_text: We camp near the river during the dry season so the cattle can drink easily.
hint: Say you camp near water in dry season

english_text: The boy ran fast to bring the herd home before the dark storm arrived.
hint: Describe rushing cattle home before a storm

english_text: Good pasture with clean water nearby is worth more than gold to a herder.
hint: Say good pasture and water are most valuable

english_text: The salt lick brings all the animals together from across the wide plain.
hint: Describe animals gathering at a salt lick

english_text: After three days of walking, we reached the seasonal pasture full of green grass.
hint: Describe arriving at fresh seasonal pasture

english_text: The Fula woman sells fresh milk and sour cream at the morning market every day.
hint: Describe a Fula woman selling dairy products

english_text: The young herder learned to throw a stick with great accuracy to guide the cattle.
hint: Describe a herder learning to guide cattle

english_text: When the rainy season ends, the Fula families begin their long journey southward.
hint: Describe the start of seasonal migration

english_text: The cattle thief was caught and brought before the village elders for judgment.
hint: Describe a cattle thief being judged

english_text: A sick cow must be separated from the herd quickly before others fall ill.
hint: Say sick cattle must be kept apart

english_text: The herdsman counts his animals every evening to make sure none have strayed.
We gave the exhausted cow extra water and let her rest in the shade all day.
hint: Describe caring for a tired cow

english_text: The children help to herd the goats while the men take the cattle to pasture.
hint: Describe children helping with goat herding

english_text: A good dog trained well is worth more than three lazy men as a cattle guard.
hint: Say a good dog beats lazy guards for cattle

english_text: The water hole that many animals share must be kept clean and free of mud.
hint: Say the shared water hole must be kept clean

english_text: My grandmother used to cure animal sickness with plants she found in the bush.
hint: Describe a grandmother's traditional animal medicine

english_text: The first thing you do in the morning is greet your animals and check them.
hint: Say checking animals is the first morning task

english_text: When buying cattle at the market, look at the teeth first to judge the age.
hint: Give advice on how to judge cattle age

english_text: I do not understand what you are saying, can you please say it again?
hint: Ask someone to repeat what they said

english_text: Excuse me, I am looking for the road that goes to the central market.
hint: Ask for directions to the market

english_text: I am sorry, I did not mean to do that, it was an accident.
hint: Apologize for doing something by accident

english_text: Can you help me carry this heavy bag to my house nearby?
hint: Ask for help carrying something heavy

english_text: How do I get to the hospital from this place where I am now?
hint: Ask for directions to the hospital

english_text: I would like to buy two of those, how much will that cost me?
hint: Ask how much two items will cost

english_text: Please can you write your phone number here so I can call you later?
I am not from here, I am just visiting this town for a few days.
hint: Explain you are a visitor to the town

english_text: What time does the morning bus leave from this station to the next town?
I need to charge my phone, do you have a place where I can do that?
hint: Ask if you can charge your phone somewhere

english_text: My bag is missing and I think I left it on the bus this morning.
hint: Report a missing bag left on a bus

english_text: I would like to exchange some money, where is the money changer here?
Can you speak more slowly please, I am still learning your language?
hint: Ask someone to speak slower while learning

english_text: I am looking for somewhere to sleep tonight, is there a guesthouse near here?
hint: Ask about a place to sleep nearby

english_text: Do you have something smaller than this note to give me as change?
hint: Ask for change for a large note

english_text: I am feeling very unwell today, I think I need to see a doctor soon.
hint: Say you feel sick and need a doctor

english_text: Could you please tell me your name again, I forgot what you said before?
hint: Ask someone to repeat their name

english_text: Is there anyone here who can speak a little English to help me please?
hint: Ask if anyone speaks English to help

english_text: I want to send this to my family but I do not know how much it costs.
hint: Say you want to send something but need cost

english_text: I have been waiting here for more than one hour and no one has come.
hint: Complain about waiting a long time

english_text: This is not what I ordered, I asked for something different from this.
hint: Complain you got the wrong order

english_text: I cannot eat this food because I do not eat meat for religious reasons.
hint: Explain you cannot eat meat for religion

english_text: My child is very sick with a high fever and I am very worried about her.
hint: Say your child has a fever and you're worried

english_text: Please call an ambulance immediately, there has been a bad accident here.
hint: Ask someone to call emergency services

english_text: I left my passport at the hotel, can you wait for me while I go to get it?
hint: Ask someone to wait while you get your passport

english_text: Can you show me where the toilet is in this building please?
I want to make a phone call, do you have a phone I can borrow for a moment?
hint: Ask to borrow someone's phone for a call

english_text: I do not have enough money right now, can I pay you the rest tomorrow?
hint: Ask to pay the remaining money tomorrow

english_text: How long will it take to get to the border crossing by bus from here?
hint: Ask how long the bus journey to the border takes

english_text: I need to find a mechanic because my motorbike broke down on the road.
hint: Say your motorbike broke down and you need help

english_text: Could you please hold my bag for a moment while I look for my money?
hint: Ask someone to hold your bag briefly

english_text: Is this seat taken or is it free for me to sit down here with you?
hint: Ask if a seat is available to sit

english_text: I want to go to the main mosque for Friday prayers, which way is it?
hint: Ask for directions to the Friday mosque

english_text: Do you know someone who can help me translate this letter into French?
hint: Ask for help translating a letter to French

english_text: I need to find a pharmacy that is still open at this time of night.
hint: Say you need a pharmacy open at night

english_text: Can you help me read this form because my eyesight is not very good?
hint: Ask for help reading a form

english_text: I missed the last bus and now I need to find somewhere to spend the night.
hint: Say you missed the bus and need accommodation

english_text: Please let the doctor know that I have been waiting here since early morning.
hint: Ask to tell the doctor you've been waiting long

english_text: I want to register my child for school, who is the person I should speak to?
hint: Ask who to talk to about enrolling a child in school
